Edge.description: A directed relationship between two Nodes.

access_type.description: Access direction on ACCESSES edges: read | write | readwrite

Edge.title: Edge

Evidence.description: A single piece of detection evidence supporting a Node.

detail.description: Detection description: '<adapter>: <evidence_kind>' for PROMPT nodes (full content is in metadata.extras.content); '<adapter>: <snippet>' (up to 500 chars) for all other node types.
detail.title: Detail

Evidence.title: Evidence

Node.description: A detected AI component (agent, model, tool, prompt, datastore, etc.).
Node.title: Node

NodeMetadata.description: Typed + open-ended metadata attached to a Node.

model_name.description: LLM / embedding model name if applicable
model_name.title: Model Name

privilege_scope.description: Privilege or permission scope label, e.g. 'db_write', 'filesystem_read'
privilege_scope.title: Privilege Scope

endpoint.description: API endpoint address, e.g. '0.0.0.0:8080 (sse)' for MCP or '/chat' for REST
endpoint.title: Endpoint

transport.description: Transport protocol for API/MCP nodes, e.g. 'sse', 'streamable-http', 'stdio'
transport.title: Transport

server_name.description: Server display name (MCP FastMCP name kwarg, or inferred service name)
server_name.title: Server Name

deployment_target.description: Cloud or container deployment target, e.g. 'aws', 'gcp', 'kubernetes'
deployment_target.title: Deployment Target

data_classification.description: PII/PHI classification labels detected in schemas stored in this datastore, e.g. ['PHI', 'PII'].  Null when no classified fields were found.
data_classification.title: Data Classification

classified_tables.description: SQL table or Python model names within this datastore that carry PII/PHI fields.
classified_tables.title: Classified Tables

classified_fields.description: Per-table/-model mapping of sensitive field names to their classification labels, e.g. {'patients': ['name', 'dob'], 'users': ['email', 'password']}.
classified_fields.title: Classified Fields

secret_store.description: Secret management service in use, e.g. 'aws_secrets_manager', 'azure_key_vault', 'gcp_secret_manager', 'hashicorp_vault', 'k8s_secret'
secret_store.title: Secret Store

encryption_at_rest.description: True when encryption-at-rest is explicitly configured in IaC
encryption_at_rest.title: Encryption At Rest

encryption_key_ref.description: KMS key ARN, Key Vault URI, or CMEK resource reference
encryption_key_ref.title: Encryption Key Ref

runs_as_root.description: True when the container is configured to run as root (UID 0); False = non-root
runs_as_root.title: Runs As Root

has_health_check.description: True when a HEALTHCHECK instruction (Dockerfile) or liveness/readiness probe (K8s) is present
has_health_check.title: Has Health Check

has_resource_limits.description: True when Kubernetes resource limits are defined for the workload container
has_resource_limits.title: Has Resource Limits

principal.description: ARN, email, or object ID of the IAM principal
principal.title: Principal

permissions.description: Actions or scopes granted by this IAM entity (up to 20 entries)
permissions.title: Permissions

trust_principals.description: Principals trusted to assume this role (AWS trust policy subjects, K8s binding subjects)
trust_principals.title: Trust Principals

description.description: Human-readable description of this component. For TOOL nodes: extracted from function docstring; used by the redteam test generator to craft context-authentic parameter injection payloads. For AGENT nodes: extracted from the agent's purpose/role description.
description.title: Description

parameters.description: For TOOL nodes: list of parameters accepted by this tool, extracted from the function signature and docstring. Used by TOOL_ABUSE test scenarios to craft parameter-injection payloads targeting each parameter.
parameters.title: Parameters

injection_risk_score.description: For AGENT nodes: pre-computed injection risk score in [0, 1] set by the graph enricher. Derived from: number of tools with no_auth_required or high_privilege, presence of unguarded paths, reachable PII datastores, and unguarded HITL triggers. Used by ScenarioGenerator to sort generated test scenarios by expected impact.
injection_risk_score.title: Injection Risk Score

mcp_server_url.description: URL of the MCP server this tool belongs to, e.g. 'http://mcp.example.com'
mcp_server_url.title: Mcp Server Url

trust_level.description: MCP server trust classification set by the graph enricher: 'trusted' | 'untrusted' | 'n/a'. All external MCP servers are 'untrusted' unless listed in redteam.mcp_trusted_servers in nuguard.yaml.
trust_level.title: Trust Level

no_auth_required.description: True when the tool or endpoint is invocable without authentication
no_auth_required.title: No Auth Required

high_privilege.description: True when the tool has access to administrative or cross-tenant resources
high_privilege.title: High Privilege

sql_injectable.description: True when the tool constructs SQL queries from agent-provided string parameters
sql_injectable.title: Sql Injectable

ssrf_possible.description: True when the tool accepts URL parameters that are fetched server-side (SSRF surface)
ssrf_possible.title: Ssrf Possible

accepts_external_url.description: True when the tool has a URL/endpoint parameter that is passed to an outbound request
accepts_external_url.title: Accepts External Url

reads_external_content.description: True when the tool fetches or reads content from an external source (web, email, GitHub, etc.)
reads_external_content.title: Reads External Content

system_prompt_excerpt.description: First 500 characters of the agent's system prompt / instructions / backstory. Used by the red-team scenario generator to craft context-authentic payloads.
system_prompt_excerpt.title: System Prompt Excerpt

rules_excerpt.description: Short description of the guardrail's rules, validator class, or blocking logic
rules_excerpt.title: Rules Excerpt

blocked_topics.description: Topics this guardrail is known to block (extracted by enricher or adapter)
blocked_topics.title: Blocked Topics

blocked_actions.description: Actions this guardrail is known to block (extracted by enricher or adapter)
blocked_actions.title: Blocked Actions

refusal_style.description: How this guardrail normally refuses: 'hard_block', 'redirect', 'warn', etc.
refusal_style.title: Refusal Style

auth_required.description: True when this API endpoint requires authentication
auth_required.title: Auth Required

auth_scope.description: OAuth2 scope or role required to call this endpoint: 'user' | 'admin' | 'none'
auth_scope.title: Auth Scope

accepts_user_input.description: True when this endpoint accepts user-controlled input in body or query params
accepts_user_input.title: Accepts User Input

returns_sensitive_data.description: True when this endpoint returns PII, PHI, or other sensitive data
returns_sensitive_data.title: Returns Sensitive Data

rate_limited.description: True when rate limiting is configured for this endpoint
rate_limited.title: Rate Limited

idor_surface.description: True when this endpoint has user- or tenant-scoped path parameters such as {user_id} or {tenant_id}
idor_surface.title: Idor Surface

path_params.description: Path parameter names extracted from the endpoint URL template, e.g. ['user_id', 'tenant_id']
path_params.title: Path Params

request_body_schema.description: Pydantic/dataclass field map for the request body: {field_name: type_string}
request_body_schema.title: Request Body Schema

chat_payload_key.description: Inferred primary prompt field in the request body (e.g. 'message', 'query')
chat_payload_key.title: Chat Payload Key

chat_payload_list.description: True when the chat payload key is typed as a list (e.g. list[str])
chat_payload_list.title: Chat Payload List

response_text_key.description: Inferred primary response text field in the response body
response_text_key.title: Response Text Key

pii_fields.description: Flat list of PII field names in this datastore, e.g. ['name', 'email', 'ssn']. Derived from classified_fields by the graph enricher.
pii_fields.title: Pii Fields

phi_fields.description: Flat list of PHI field names in this datastore, e.g. ['diagnosis', 'medication', 'lab_result']. Derived from classified_fields by the graph enricher.
phi_fields.title: Phi Fields

access_type.description: Datastore access mode inferred from ACCESSES edge: 'read' | 'write' | 'readwrite'
access_type.title: Access Type

NodeMetadata.title: NodeMetadata

PackageDep.description: A single declared package dependency (Python or JavaScript).
PackageDep.title: PackageDep

ScanSummary.description: Deterministic scan-level summary populated on every extraction.

use_case.description: Human-readable description of the application's AI use cases
use_case.title: Use Case

modality_support.description: Detailed modality flags, e.g. {'text': true, 'voice': false}
modality_support.title: Modality Support

api_endpoints.description: API route paths extracted from source (e.g. ['/chat', '/health'])
api_endpoints.title: Api Endpoints

deployment_platforms.description: Cloud/CI platforms inferred from IaC files (e.g. ['AWS', 'GCP'])
deployment_platforms.title: Deployment Platforms

deployment_urls.description: Canonical deployment URLs found in IaC/workflow files
deployment_urls.title: Deployment Urls

iac_accounts.description: Cloud account IDs / subscription IDs / project IDs found in IaC
iac_accounts.title: Iac Accounts

node_counts.description: Count of nodes per ComponentType, e.g. {'AGENT': 3, 'MODEL': 2}
node_counts.title: Node Counts

secret_stores.description: Deduped secret management services referenced across all IaC files
secret_stores.title: Secret Stores

availability_zones.description: All cloud availability zones referenced in IaC files
availability_zones.title: Availability Zones

encryption_at_rest_coverage.description: True when at least one IaC resource has encryption-at-rest configured
encryption_at_rest_coverage.title: Encryption At Rest Coverage

security_findings.description: Notable security / resilience findings across IaC and container config, e.g. ['container_runs_as_root', 'missing_health_check', 'no_resource_limits', 'secrets_in_env_vars', 'overly_permissive_iam']
security_findings.title: Security Findings

iam_principals.description: IAM role ARNs, GCP service account emails, and Azure managed identity names
iam_principals.title: Iam Principals

service_accounts.description: K8s ServiceAccount names and GCP/Azure service account identifiers
service_accounts.title: Service Accounts

iac_security_summary.description: LLM-generated security briefing for practitioners covering deployment posture, IAM configuration, secret management, encryption, HA, and CI/CD risks across all detected IaC and GitHub Actions workflows.
iac_security_summary.title: Iac Security Summary

data_classification.description: Union of all data classification labels detected across the repository, e.g. ['PHI', 'PII'].  Empty list when no classified fields are found.
data_classification.title: Data Classification

classified_tables.description: Names of SQL tables or Python models that contain classified data fields (PII or PHI).  Sorted alphabetically.
classified_tables.title: Classified Tables

startup_commands.description: Startup commands discovered from package.json, Makefile, Procfile, pyproject.toml, or inferred entry points.  Each entry is {command, source, label} where label is 'dev' or 'start'.
startup_commands.title: Startup Commands

env_files.description: Relative paths to .env / dotenv files found in the repository.
env_files.title: Env Files

env_var_keys.description: Sorted list of environment variable *keys* found across all dotenv files. Values are intentionally omitted from the SBOM.
env_var_keys.title: Env Var Keys

local_url.description: Inferred local dev URL (e.g. 'http://localhost:8000') derived from PORT env var or startup command hints.  None when not determinable.
local_url.title: Local Url

staging_urls.description: Staging / QA deployment URLs discovered from env files or config.
staging_urls.title: Staging Urls

production_urls.description: Production deployment URLs discovered from env files or config.
production_urls.title: Production Urls

log_paths.description: Log file paths discovered during scanning (relative to app root).
log_paths.title: Log Paths

ScanSummary.title: ScanSummary

SourceLocation.description: File/line pointer for a piece of evidence.

path.description: Relative path to the source file
path.title: Path

SourceLocation.title: SourceLocation

ToolParameter.description:
Schema for a single parameter accepted by a TOOL node.

Captured from function signatures and docstrings by the SBOM extractor.
Used by the redteam test generator to craft parameter-injection payloads
for TOOL_ABUSE scenarios.

name.description: Parameter name as it appears in the function signature
name.title: Name

description.description: Parameter description extracted from the function docstring
description.title: Description

required.description: True when the parameter has no default value
required.title: Required

ToolParameter.title: ToolParameter

description:
AI Bill of Materials document produced by Xelo.

This is the canonical output format.  Use ``AiSbomSerializer.to_json()``
to serialise and ``AiSbomDocument.model_validate()`` to parse and validate.

schema_version.description: AIBOM schema version (semver); bump when format changes
schema_version.title: Schema Version

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this document was generated
generated_at.title: Generated At

target.description: Repository URL or local path that was scanned
target.title: Target

deps.description: Package dependencies from manifests (pyproject.toml, requirements*.txt, package.json, …)
deps.title: Deps

summary.description: Scan-level metadata: use-case summary, frameworks, modalities, API endpoints, and IaC/deployment context

title: AiSbomDocument
